mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through mechanical repricing of equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation between 1-month FEDFUNDS change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings expectations and driving positive equity returns. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of more than 100,000 persons triggers positive 20-day forward returns through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series rise simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 60 trading days due to multiplier effects on corporate revenues and profit margins. The interaction captures demand momentum that single-series signals miss.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal produces positive returns in fewer than 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO over 2 months signals supply-side weakness and stagflationary pressure, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth and inflation risk simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) interact to signal labor market tightness. When UNRATE falls while PAYEMS rises, wage pressure and labor cost inflation accelerate, compressing profit margins and producing negative 20-day forward returns in equity markets sensitive to margin compression.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative returns in fewer than 54% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals restrictive monetary policy in an inflationary regime. This regime predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real rates remain elevated and growth expectations compress.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and construction employment by 2-3 months. A month-over-month rise in HOUST > 5% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through forward-looking expectations of demand acceleration and multiplier effects.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure aggregate demand momentum. When both accelerate (GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized and RSAFS growth > 0.3% month-over-month), equity returns are positive over 60 trading days due to broad-based demand expansion and earnings growth visibility.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal produces positive returns in fewer than 57% of instances.
